author: haiku-memory-surface <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

Added three memory-surface properties to `crates/jjf-storage/tests/proptest_harness.rs`:
- `memory_round_trip_set_then_unset` (lines ~778-805): set then read returns `Some(Memory{key, value})`; after unset, read returns `None`.
- `memory_idempotent_set_and_unset` (lines ~807-848): two `set_memory(k, v)` with same value leave key/value/created_at stable; first unset succeeds, second on missing key surfaces typed `Error::Invalid` (the storage contract — `unset` is NOT silently-ok on missing).
- `memory_set_does_not_affect_other_keys` (lines ~850-883): `prop_assume!(k1 != k2)`; seeded k2, mutated k1, re-read k2 unchanged; also asserts k1 actually landed.

Generators (lines ~487-538): 5-element `MEMORY_KEY_POOL` of kebab-case slugs (`alpha-key` ... `epsilon-key`, all passing `validate_slug`'s 3-48 char rule), values built as `'x' + [0,255]` non-control ASCII so `set_memory`'s "non-empty after trim" gate always passes.

## Recommendation

Land as-is. Properties exercise the cache-rebuild path on memory keys (the same bug class that bit issue snapshots).

## Confidence

High. Wall-clock: 15.72s for the full harness file (target ~35s). `cargo nextest run --workspace`: 623 tests pass.

## Open follow-ups

None. Closes the qa-proptest-followup label as far as memory goes.
